name: Lemon Liqueur
recipeInstructions:
1. Thinly peel the zest strips from the lemons, excluding the white pith. Place the zest strips in a medium saucepan.
2. Cut the lemons in half and squeeze the juice into a measuring cup, removing any seeds. Measure the juice and add enough water to make 1 cup. Pour the lemon juice into the saucepan with the zest.
3. Add sugar and stir. Bring the mixture to a boil, stirring frequently.
4. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes when it reaches a boil. Remove from heat and let cool.
5. Pour the lemon mixture into an aging container, add vodka, and stir. Cap and age for 4 weeks in a cool, dark place.
6. After initial aging, strain the liqueur through a fine cloth (such as muslin) placed over a bowl. Repeat as needed. A cloudy layer may form on top after several strainings; this portion can be poured off and reserved for cooking if desired.
7. Bottle and cap the liqueur as desired. The liqueur is ready to use in cooking but is preferred for drinking after 3 or more months of aging. The liqueur will be ready for drinking within 3 to 5 months.
